div.head: The Chargé in Iraq (Satterthwaite) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 34. Department’s circular September 27, 4 p.m. The Acting Minister for Foreign Affairs tells me that in the event of a general war Iraq will carry out fully its obligations under Article IV of its Treaty of Alliance with Great BritainSigned October 10, 1922; British and Foreign State Papers, vol. cxix, p. 389. as well as those under the League Covenant and the Saadabad Pact.Signed at Teheran, July 8, 1937, by Afghanistan, Iraq, Iran, and Turkey; League of Nations Treaty Series, vol. cxc, p. 21. The four Foreign Ministers of the latter are now in Geneva and can consult there. Public sympathy seems to have turned toward Great Britain and the Government has not even mentioned the Palestine situation to the British Embassy in assuring it of support.
I anticipate no disorders and think American citizens here and in the Persian Gulf will be quite safe. There are ample food supplies.
